description: This figure presents a pair of bright-field transmission electron microscopy (TEM) micrographs of deformed Fe–28% Al alloy, captured under +g and g imaging conditions with a zone axis near [0 0 1] and an imaging vector of type 1 1 0. The scale bar indicates 0.2 μm. The left micrograph shows the general dislocation structure, with an arrow pointing to a specific region of interest. The right micrograph, taken under the opposite g condition, reveals straight and parallel reaction dislocations explicitly marked with 'r' labels, which are identified as sessile <100> edge dislocations formed by the reaction of two <111> superdislocations. These reaction products act as strong barriers to further glide of <111> superdislocations. The images demonstrate that at this composition—corresponding to the peak work hardening behavior in Fe–Al alloys—the dislocation structure is dominated by straight screw-oriented superdislocations (composed of two (1/2)<111> partials) and their reaction products. The complementary +g/g imaging confirms the nature of these reaction dislocations and supports the mechanistic interpretation that the anomalous peak work hardening at ~28% Al arises from elastic anisotropy-enhanced Peierls forces and the formation of sessile dislocation locks, rather than from APB tube mechanisms.
